title: rudbeckia angustifolia
intro: Rudbeckia angustifolia, also known as Black-eyed Susan, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and dark centers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. Rudbeckia angustifolia is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a range of applications.
